reasoning: The VA 30-year mortgage rate recently experienced a huge, sudden spike (from ~5.9% to 6.556% over just eight days) driven by bond market reactions to potential new tariffs and inflation fears. Because 6.556% represents a sharp local peak (and a Friday high), short-term mean reversion makes it more likely than not that rates will pull back slightly by April 20. Therefore, the short-term probability of being even higher (>6.556%) on April 20 is around 32%. Over longer horizons, uncertainty increases and the possibility that rates settle into a new, higher structural channel (if tariff proposals solidify into policy) grows. As a result, the probability pushes closer to the uninformative 50% baseline for the later dates in July and October.
